steps.action_description: Cube chicken into bite-sized pieces. Dice onion and peppers. Slice celery and radishes. Mince garlic. Thaw spinach in microwave for 2 mins, then squeeze bone-dry in a kitchen towel. Zest and juice the lemon.

meanwhile.action: Mix yogurt with lemon zest and 1 tbsp of the lemon juice in a small bowl. Set aside.

steps.action_description: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Season chicken with salt and pepper. Sear until golden-brown on all sides (about 5-6 mins). It doesn't need to be fully cooked yet. Remove chicken to a plate.
Add remaining oil to the same skillet. Sauté onion, peppers, and celery until they begin to soften (4 mins). Stir in the garlic and cook for 1 min until fragrant.

meanwhile.action: Rinse and drain if not already done.

steps.action_description: Add chickpeas, all dry spices, and bouillon to the pan. Stir to coat. Return the chicken and any juices to the pan along with the squeezed spinach and a splash of water (2-3 tbsp). Cover and simmer for 5-6 mins until chicken is cooked through.

REDUCE HEAT TO LOW. Stir in the butter until melted. Gently fold in the yogurt/lemon mixture. Stir constantly for 1-2 mins until a creamy sauce forms. Do not let it boil! Adjust seasoning with salt, pepper, and more lemon juice if needed.
Spoon into bowls. Garnish with sliced radishes for crunch and a dusting of Parmesan. Serve immediately.

meanwhile.action: PULL: If kids want plain components, save some seared chicken and chickpeas before the spice/yogurt steps.
